title: AN ACT INCREASING THE BED CAPACITY OF THE PAQUIBATO EMERGENCY HOSPITAL IN THE DISTRICT OF PAQUIBATO, DAVAO CITY, FROM TEN (10) TO TWENTY-FIVE (25) BEDS, UPGRADING IT INTO A DISTRICT HOSPITAL TO BE KNOWN AS THE PAQUIBATO DISTRICT HOSPITAL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 30385 / 88 OG No. 15, 1971 (Apr. 13, 1992)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7216, March 06, 1992 ]

AN ACT INCREASING THE BED CAPACITY OF THE PAQUIBATO EMERGENCY HOSPITAL IN THE DISTRICT OF PAQUIBATO, DAVAO CITY, FROM TEN (10) TO TWENTY-FIVE (25) BEDS, UPGRADING IT INTO A DISTRICT HOSPITAL TO BE KNOWN AS THE PAQUIBATO DISTRICT HOSPITAL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. The bed capacity of the Paquibato Emergency Hospital in the District of Paquibato, Davao City, is hereby increased from ten (10) to twenty-five (25) beds and upgrading it into a district hospital to be known as the Paquibato District Hospital.

SEC. 2. The amount necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act shall be included in the General Appropriations Act of the year following its enactment into law.

SEC. 3. This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved,

(Sgd.)

NEPTALI A. GONZALES

(Sgd.)

RAMON V. MITRA

President of the Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on March 12, 1991 and December 11, 1991, respectively.

(Sgd.)

ANACLETO D. BADOY, JR.

(Sgd.)

CAMILO L. SABIO

Secretary of the Senate

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: March 6, 1992

(Sgd.)

CORAZON C. AQUINO

President of the Philippines
